title: What is Conditional Life? | HG Amitasana Dasa
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So here in this verse difference between conditional life and life in Krishna
consciousness is being explained when the living entity is attracted by the
three modes of material nature. It is called conditional life. So here Prabhupada
mentions that Ganeshu or Maya consciousness involves attachment to the
three material modes of nature under which one works sometimes in goodness
and knowledge sometimes in passion and sometime in ignorance. Krishna in the
Bhagavad Gita mentions that as living entities we are his amshas
and we are of the same quality of the Supreme Sat, Chit and
Ananda. But if we analyze our present state it is quite in contrast to our
original quality of being Sat, Chit and Ananda. Everyone is struggling.
Some have realized it. Some are so much in ignorance that they don't even realize
it. Prabhupada gives example of a Hog living in a filthy place. Apparently it is
happy it doesn't even recognize or realize that it is in such abominable
condition. Sometimes we see people, you know, completely dirty, not taken bath,
completely torn clothes, sometimes beggars
or
People living in slums.
I mean, when we see that, it's difficult for us to comprehend how they can live like that.
Such dirty clothes, not taken bath, body is full of, you know, you can see physically black layer on the skin.
We can't even contemplate living like this even for half a day.
So, that is the meaning of conditioning.
Conditioning means slowly you get used to.
